{% load i18n %}

{% trans 'This guide will walk you through setting up automated alerts on TradingView, helping you streamline your trading process and save time.' %}

{% trans 'Getting Started' %}

{% trans 'Visit' %} TradingView {% trans 'and open a chart from the' %} {% trans 'Product -> Supercharts' %} {% trans 'section. To automate your alerts, use a strategy or indicator that supports built-in alerts. We recommend the IsAlgo strategy for its integrated alert system.' %}

{% trans 'Step 1: Configure Your Chart' %}

{% trans '1. Select the Asset:' %} {% trans 'Choose the asset to trade (e.g., BTCUSD, NAS100) and ensure the correct timeframe is displayed.' %}

{% trans '2. Add the Strategy:' %} {% trans 'Apply your strategy to the chart. Test it thoroughly before automating your alerts.' %}

{% trans 'Step 2: Writing Alerts' %}

{% trans 'Alerts require specific inputs. Here’s how to structure them:' %}

{% trans 'Entry Alerts' %}

{% trans 'Inputs for entry alerts:' %}
• D:{% trans 'Direction (Buy or Sell)' %}
• A:{% trans 'Asset name (e.g., BTCUSD, NAS100)' %}
• V:{% trans 'Volume or trade amount' %}
• ID:{% trans 'Trade ID' %}({% trans 'use' %} {{ '{{ID}' }}} {% trans 'with IsAlgo' %})

{% trans 'Example Entry Alerts:' %}
• Long: D=Buy A=BTCUSD V=10 ID={{ '{{ID}' }}}
• Short: D=Sell A=BTCUSD V=10 ID={{ '{{ID}' }}}

{% trans 'Exit Alerts' %}

{% trans 'Inputs for exit alerts:' %}
• X:{% trans 'Trade type to exit (Buy or Sell)' %}
• A:{% trans 'Asset name' %}
• P:{% trans 'Partial close percentage' %}({% trans 'use' %} {{ '{{partial_close}' }}} {% trans 'with IsAlgo' %})
• ID:{% trans 'Trade ID' %}({% trans 'use' %} {{ '{{ID}' }}} {% trans 'with IsAlgo' %})

{% trans 'Example Exit Alerts:' %}
• Long Exit: X=Buy A=BTCUSD P={{ '{{partial_close}' }}} ID={{ '{{ID}' }}}
• Short Exit: X=Sell A=BTCUSD P={{ '{{partial_close}' }}} ID={{ '{{ID}' }}}

{% trans 'Step 3: Paste Alerts' %}

{% trans 'If using IsAlgo, paste the alert messages into the relevant input fields: Entry Long, Entry Short, Exit Long, Exit Short. Click OK to save.' %}

{% trans 'Step 4: Create a TradingView Alert' %}

{% trans 'To create a TradingView alert:' %}

  1. {% trans 'Click the Alert icon (bell) in the chart toolbar.' %}
  2. {% trans 'Select' %} {% trans 'Create Alert' %}.
  3. {% trans 'In' %} {% trans 'Condition' %},{% trans 'choose your strategy/indicator and select' %} alert() function calls only.
  4. {% trans 'Give the alert a descriptive name.' %}
  5. {% trans 'Go to the' %} {% trans 'Notifications' %} {% trans 'tab, enable' %} {% trans 'Webhook URL' %},{% trans 'and paste your Webhook URL.' %}
  6. {% trans 'Click' %} {% trans 'Create' %}.

{% trans 'Note:' %} {% trans 'Alerts are tied to the current asset and timeframe. Changing these or modifying strategy inputs requires recreating the alert.' %}
